content:
7 Best Litter Boxes for Large Cats – Jumbo Picks That Actually Fit (2026)

Standard litter boxes are designed for 8–10 lb cats. If you have a Maine Coon, Ragdoll, Norwegian Forest Cat, or any large breed, those boxes are too small — leading to accidents, overspray, and litter scatter. We tested 7 jumbo litter boxes with cats weighing 15–25 lbs to find which ones actually provide enough turning room and comfort.

Why Large Cats Need Bigger Litter Boxes

The veterinary rule of thumb is that a litter box should be 1.5× your cat's length (nose to tail base). A 20-inch Maine Coon needs a box at least 30 inches long. Most standard boxes are 18–22 inches — far too small. Cramped boxes cause large cats to hang over edges, spray outside walls, and avoid the box entirely, leading to house soiling problems that owners mistakenly attribute to behavioral issues.

Large cats also produce more waste, requiring deeper litter to maintain absorption between scoopings. A minimum depth of 6 inches prevents large cats from digging to the bottom and scratching the plastic floor, which creates odor-trapping grooves.

The DIY Storage Bin Hack

Many large cat owners swear by the storage bin method: buy a 66-quart or larger clear storage bin, cut a U-shaped entry on one side (7–8 inches high), and sand the edges smooth. This creates the largest possible interior space at the lowest cost. A $15 storage bin provides more usable space than most $60 commercial jumbo litter boxes.

The downsides are less aesthetic and no odor-control features, but for pure function and large cat comfort, nothing beats an oversized storage bin with 6 inches of quality clumping litter.

Key Features for Large Cat Litter Boxes

  • Interior length: Minimum 28", ideal 32"+ for Maine Coons
  • Interior width: Minimum 17", ideal 19"+ for comfortable turning
  • Wall height: 12"+ to contain spray from standing urination
  • Entry height: 7–9" for easy access (lower for senior large cats)
  • Material: Thick, stain-resistant plastic that won't absorb odors
  • Base stability: Non-slip feet prevent sliding on hard floors

Litter Recommendations for Large Cats

Large cats need 4–6 inches of litter depth for proper digging and covering. Use fine-grain clumping litter for best absorption with high-volume waste. Expect to use 15–20 lbs of litter per box per month for a single large cat. Scoop twice daily to maintain odor control with large cat waste volumes.

Frequently Asked Questions

What size litter box does a Maine Coon need?

At minimum 28 inches long and 17 inches wide. Ideally 32"+ long for full-size Maine Coons. The box should be 1.5× your cat's length from nose to tail base for comfortable turning and digging.

Are top-entry litter boxes good for large cats?

Only if the opening is at least 10 inches in diameter. Some large cats struggle with standard 9-inch top openings. Senior large cats should avoid top-entry boxes due to the climbing requirement.

How often should I clean a litter box for a large cat?

Scoop twice daily and do a full litter change every 2 weeks. Large cats produce more waste and odor, so more frequent maintenance is essential for both hygiene and your cat's willingness to use the box.
